meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kit: Overcome 'No Project Ideas' for Students
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on your portfolio. Use this SaaS starter kit checklist with Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade projects as a student.
content.answer_block: The 'no project idea' paralysis ends by shifting from invention to infrastructure. Use a standardized SaaS starter kit to build a robust foundation—authentication, database, and billing—before adding your unique feature layer. This converts a blank screen into a repeatable, high-value asset that showcases your technical architecture skills to employers.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking rhythmically like a countdown timer. You have the technical skills to build, but the 'what' remains a phantom, keeping your portfolio empty and your confidence stagnant. This is the silent killer of engineering careers: the inability to bridge the gap between syntax and a functional, live-market product.

But here is the secret: top-tier builders don't wait for divine inspiration. They build engines, not just cars. By leveraging a high-performance SaaS starter kit, you stop worrying about the 'idea' and start mastering the architecture of deployment. It’s time to stop theorizing and start shipping.

steps.title: Define the Foundational Stack
steps.description: Establish a robust baseline using a modern stack (Next.js, Supabase, Tailwind). This ensures your project is production-ready from commit one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modern SaaS project structure using Next.js 14, App Router, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. Include a clean folder architecture for components, hooks, and server actions.

steps.title: Implement Modular Authentication
steps.description: Don't reinvent the wheel. Use existing auth providers to handle secure sessions, allowing you to focus on the core user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add authentication logic using Supabase Auth. Create a protected route wrapper and a user profile schema in the database.

steps.title: Architect the Database Layer
steps.description: Define your data models early to avoid technical debt. Focus on scalability and relational integrity between users, subscriptions, and app-specific data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational database schema for a SaaS app including tables for Users, Subscriptions, and Projects. Use Prisma for ORM integration.

steps.title: Integrate Monetization Infrastructure
steps.description: Even if your project is free, implementing Stripe billing proves you understand the full lifecycle of a commercial SaaS application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement Stripe checkout sessions and webhook handlers to manage subscription tiers. Ensure the database updates correctly upon successful payment.

steps.title: Deploy and Automate
steps.description: A project isn't real until it's live. Automate your deployment pipeline to ensure every push triggers a build and test cycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, type-checking, and deploys the application to Vercel upon merging to the main branch.

pro_tips: Always build for 'multi-tenancy' from day one; it forces you to write cleaner, more scalable database queries.
Prioritize 'Time to First Commit'—don't spend more than 30 minutes configuring your environment; use a boilerplate.
Focus on solving one micro-problem for a specific niche rather than building a generic 'productivity app'.
Use Claude Code to document your code as you write it; this makes your GitHub repo look professional to recruiters.
Master the 'Stripe Webhook' flow; it is the most impressive technical detail you can put on a student resume.
content.next_steps: After completing your first SaaS build, focus on adding one 'Killer Feature' that utilizes an LLM API to differentiate your project from generic student tutorials.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build professional software that stands out in a sea of basic tutorials. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the infrastructure that makes every idea possible. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
